name: Swedish Midsommar Celebration
snippet: Celebrate midsummer Swedish style!
details:
Celebrate midsummer Swedish style!

The evening will start with everyone helping decorate the Swedish maypole with the support of Featherstone Flowers. Then, there will be a floral crown making opportunity for children and grown-ups, followed by Swedish songs around the maypole performed by Stockbridge Primary School. Local folk duo, Confluence, will play Swedish music, there will be meatballs and Swedish fika on sale with our local Stuga Swedish Bakery, and then Irish shanty music with ShantyHead to create a rousing end to the evening. Bring a picnic, rugs or tables and chairs.

Tickets free – but please book your place online

name: Watercolour Well-being Workshop
snippet: Explore free-flowing painting techniques & breathing meditations.
details:
Lisa Gardner is a watercolour artist inspired by the natural world, the connection between breath and brushwork, and rare wildflower species on the edge of extinction.

Explore free-flowing painting techniques & breathing meditations to nurture your creativity and inspire calm. After a tour of the flower field and a chance to pick a vase of flowers, Lisa will take you through how to draw on your creative voice, trust your intuition and be inspired by vibrant seasonal botanicals.

Tickets £75

name: Exhibition in the garden by The Wessex Sculptors
snippet: Enjoy the varied and skilled work of the Wessex Sculptors.
details:
Enjoy the varied and skilled work of the Wessex Sculptors displayed in the little-known gardens of The Quaker Meeting House off Colebrook Street. 10 artists will be on display.

There will also be artists sketching and painting the gardens on certain days.

Free Entry

snippet: Contemporary art exhibition with outdoor sculpture trail.
details:
An exhibition of contemporary art, including painting, sculpture and ceramics with an outdoor sculpture trail. Set in the house and gardens of a former Victorian rectory.

Free admission

name: The Gatehouse Studios Artists’ Exhibition and Workshops
snippet: Local artists exhibition in a unique garden setting.
details:
Enjoy the work of 9 eclectic local artists displaying in this unique location and garden at the entrance to the cemetery. With Suna Imre, Eileen White, Iris Hill, Georgie Carrs, Barry Gower, Lisa Orlandi, Janet Price, Noel Perkins, and Tori McLean.

Free entry

name: The Gatehouse Studios Artists’ Exhibition and Workshops
snippet: Local artists exhibition in a unique garden setting.
details:
Enjoy the work of 9 eclectic local artists displaying in this unique location and garden at the entrance to the cemetery. With Suna Imre, Eileen White, Iris Hill, Georgie Carrs, Barry Gower, Lisa Orlandi, Janet Price, Noel Perkins, and Tori McLean.

Free entry

name: Festival in the Garden
snippet: Community-led garden festival with stalls, BBQ, and live music.
details:
Join us for a community-led day in this pub garden with a mix of local stalls including a face painter, locally made bread from Buriton Bakery, crafts stalls and plant stall, plus an all-day BBQ, bouncy castle and live music from Audio Pirates to round off the day.

Free entry

name: Festival in the Garden
snippet: Community-led garden festival with stalls, BBQ, and live music.
details:
Join us for a community-led day in this pub garden with a mix of local stalls including a face painter, locally made bread from Buriton Bakery, crafts stalls and plant stall, plus an all-day BBQ, bouncy castle and live music from Audio Pirates to round off the day.

Free entry

name: Flower Pressing Workshop
snippet: Learn the art of flower pressing with guided techniques.
details:
Join artist Lucy Whitfield for this three-hour workshop, to guide you through all the elements and top tips of flower pressing. Pick flowers from the field and learn how to press. Later, arrange some pre-pressed flowers to create a beautiful work of art. Cake and tea.

Tickets £85

name: Opera in the Garden
snippet: A light-hearted evening of HMS Pinafore in the garden.
details:
HMS Pinafore with Opera Anywhere.

Join us for a light-hearted evening with Opera Anywhere performing this popular Gilbert & Sullivan opera. Bring your friends, a picnic, tables and chairs and enjoy the longest day in this exceptional setting. There will be a small gift for all fathers!

Tickets £22.50 adults, £15 children. Group of 8: £160

name: Dean Garnier Garden – meet the volunteers
snippet: Meet volunteers and learn about this historic Cathedral garden.
details:
Chat to knowledgeable volunteers and learn more about this small historic garden tucked away in the Cathedral Inner Close.

Free entry

name: Farringdon Open Gardens & Farringdon Artists Trail
snippet: Open gardens, artists trail, crafts, history, teas and plant sale.
details:
Gardens event from 1.00 –5.00pm. Artists Trail 11.00 - 5.00pm.

Visit 6 gardens in a variety of sizes, styles and layouts, plus the village Community Garden. There will also be exhibitions of crafts and village history in the church. On the Artists Trail there will be a diverse selection of paintings, ceramics, prints and sculpture. Teas and also plant sale.

Free admission

name: An Introduction to Block Printing Workshop
snippet: Explore botanical block printing and design your own item.
details:
Come and spend a morning exploring the joy of block printing especially if you love botanical subjects. You will get a chance to design an item of your choice: tea towel, cushion cover or tote bag. Coffee/tea and cake included.

Tickets £45 (limited to 10 people)

name: A Guided Flower Farm Tour with Featherstone Flowers

location.street: Berry Court Farm, Church Hill, Nether Wallop

snippet: A guided tour of the flower farm with a small posy to take home.
details:
A guided tour of the flower farm with the horticultural team with each guest receiving a small posy of Hampshire grown flowers to take home.

Tickets £35

name: Musical Midsummer Soiree
snippet: An evening garden soiree with jazz, strings and song.
details:
Dress up, bring a party of friends and a picnic, and enjoy a relaxing evening in the beautiful and extensive gardens of The Deane House with music by B Natural Jazz, The Hyde String Quartet, Nancy Longland (soprano) and Sam Lester (piano). An event not to be missed!

Tickets £22.50

name: Winchester’s Biggest Garden, St Giles Hill Park. Past, present and future
snippet: Discover the history and future of St Giles Hill Park.
details:
A short walking tour to discover a deep history through to today’s green lungs - and what the future might hold - this tour reveals the stories behind the hill overlooking Winchester.

Sessions: 25 June 10.30am–12pm, 26 June 10.30am–12pm, 28 June 4pm–5.30pm.

Tickets £6 (includes light refreshments)

name: Winchester’s Biggest Garden, St Giles Hill Park. Past, present and future
snippet: Discover the history and future of St Giles Hill Park.
details:
A short walking tour to discover a deep history through to today’s green lungs - and what the future might hold - this tour reveals the stories behind the hill overlooking Winchester.

Sessions: 25 June 10.30am–12pm, 26 June 10.30am–12pm, 28 June 4pm–5.30pm.

Tickets £6 (includes light refreshments)

name: Winchester’s Biggest Garden, St Giles Hill Park. Past, present and future
snippet: Discover the history and future of St Giles Hill Park.
details:
A short walking tour to discover a deep history through to today’s green lungs - and what the future might hold - this tour reveals the stories behind the hill overlooking Winchester.

Sessions: 25 June 10.30am–12pm, 26 June 10.30am–12pm, 28 June 4pm–5.30pm.

Tickets £6 (includes light refreshments)

name: Evening Opening Contemporary Sculpture Trail, Art Gallery Summer Show & Wine Tasting
snippet: Evening sculpture exhibition with wine tasting.
details:
An exhibition of contemporary art, including painting, sculpture and ceramics with an outdoor sculpture trail. Set in the house and gardens of a former Victorian rectory.

Otium Wine Estate, Hampshire’s newest vineyard, will be doing an exclusive preview of their sparkling wines.

Free admission

name: An Introduction to Block Printing Workshop
snippet: Evening block printing workshop with prosecco and canapés.
details:
Come and spend an evening exploring the joy of block printing when you will get a chance to design an item of your choice: tea towel, cushion cover or tote bag. A glass of prosecco and canapés included.

Tickets £45 (limited to 10 people)

name: Fire Feast at the Tichborne Arms
snippet: Outdoor fire-cooked feast with BBQ and shared dishes.
details:
Fire Feasts are back at The Tichborne Arms. For one night only, we’ll be stepping outside and cooking entirely over fire.

Head Chef Tom will be firing up the Asado to serve a generous BBQ feast of beautifully cooked meats, alongside vibrant salads and warm flatbreads, all designed for sharing.

For tickets, please go to Festival web-site

name: Walk & Talk
snippet: A guided talk exploring the history and design of the garden.
details:
Enjoy a talk about this small garden in the Cathedral Inner Close. Learn about the history of the Garden, its design features, artworks, and planting followed by refreshments and the chance to chat to volunteers.

Tickets £6 limited to 20 people

name: Art In the Garden
snippet: Explore local artists working across multiple mediums.
details:
A group of local artists tucked away in studios near Black Wharf Bridge where the Itchin Navigation Canal begins. They work in a wide range of medium including painting, sculpture, printmaking and drawing.

Refreshments available.

Free entry

name: Art In the Garden
snippet: Explore local artists working across multiple mediums.
details:
A group of local artists tucked away in studios near Black Wharf Bridge where the Itchin Navigation Canal begins. They work in a wide range of medium including painting, sculpture, printmaking and drawing.

Refreshments available.

Free entry

name: Art In the Garden
snippet: Explore local artists working across multiple mediums.
details:
A group of local artists tucked away in studios near Black Wharf Bridge where the Itchin Navigation Canal begins. They work in a wide range of medium including painting, sculpture, printmaking and drawing.

Refreshments available.

Free entry

name: Medstead Gardens & Art Experience
venue: Church House Garden and Snowdrop Studios
snippet: Explore multiple gardens, art, music, and exhibitions.
details:
Enjoy three very different, beautiful Hampshire gardens, Snowdrop Studio & Garden, Church House Gardens and the Medstead and Fours Marks Station Gardens.

Includes art, music, wine tasting, and garden experiences across multiple time slots.

name: Journey Through Clay - An experience
snippet: A creative clay experience with poetry and demonstrations.
details:
Join ceramist Lisa Stevenson for a special experience with reflections, poetry and demonstrations on the wheel and sculpture. Featuring Abigail McKern bringing poetry to life.

Sessions at 2–4pm or 6–8pm.

£15 including drinks and nibbles

name: Medstead & Fours Marks Station Garden Tours
snippet: Tour the maintained station gardens with volunteer gardeners.
details:
Join the enthusiastic and knowledgeable volunteer gardeners for tours of the meticulously maintained station gardens and learn about the fascinating history behind them. Refreshments available.

Tours at 3pm and 4.30pm.

Tickets free but please reserve a place. Numbers limited to 20 per tour.

name: Floral bloom gathering & painting workshop with Becky Clarke
snippet: Pick blooms and paint them in watercolour and ink.
details:
Author, artist and illustrator Becki Clark is joining us to lead this masterclass.

June is a perfect time to capture the delicate beauty of the farm. The workshop will begin with picking blooms from the flower field. Becki will then take you step by step through how she captures them in watercolour and ink. Tea and cake included.

Tickets £75

www.stemandgreen.co.uk

name: Willow Weaving with Louise Brown
snippet: Learn willow weaving and make a plant support.
details:
Basket maker and willow weaving expert Louise Brown will lead this masterclass.

Learn the basics from manipulating and twisting the willow to essential techniques. Together you'll make a beautiful willow plant support for flowers and plants. Or, simply statuesque shape for your border even without plants.

Tickets £85

name: Dean Garnier Garden – meet the volunteers
snippet: Meet volunteers and learn about this historic Cathedral garden.
details:
Chat to knowledgeable volunteers and learn more about this small historic garden tucked away in the Cathedral Inner Close.

Free entry

name: A Floral Extravaganza in Art
snippet: Four artists exhibiting work influenced by flowers and gardens.
details:
Come and view the new art studios in Jewry Street where 4 acclaimed artists will be exhibiting and selling their work influenced by flowers, gardens and landscapes.

The artists are Natalie Semlyen, Rebecca Galbraith, Chris Perry, and Wendy Couchman.

Free entry

name: Experimental Photography Workshop - Chemi-Lumens
snippet: Create plant-based photography chemistry from the garden.
details:
In this workshop, you will be provided with all the necessary materials to create your own chemistry from plants in The Gatehouse’s garden. The joy of using a handmade alternative to toxic commercial chemistry means the local ecology of the site is embedded into the work, as well as being far more sustainable and caring.

For full details and information on Chemi-Lumens go to: www.garden-festival.co.uk or view on our App.

Workshop £25

name: In an English Country Garden
snippet: An afternoon of performances, art, music and garden experiences.
details:
Come and admire the beautiful and extensive gardens of The Deane House while enjoying an afternoon of performances dotted around the various parts of the estate. With poetry, readings, music, art displays, ceramics workshops, well-being sessions, herbal medicine, wine tastings, drop-in painting classes – every part of the various gardens will be filled with something delightful.

Or simply bring your friends, a picnic, your tables and chairs and savour this glorious setting with magnificent views over the Hampshire countryside.

With Abigail McKern, Sian Radinger, Lisa Stevenson, Charlie, Sonya Vine, Nicola Baldwin, Coralie, Darren Black, Sarah Greenaway and Lisa Orlandi.

Tickets £15

name: The Elegant Garden Affair
snippet: An elegant garden afternoon with wine, music, fashion and tea.
details:
Experience an afternoon of refined beauty and relaxed sophistication in the delightful walled garden of the hotel. Enjoy a glass of sparkling wine on arrival, live music creating a gentle, uplifting atmosphere, a stylish and inspiring fashion show, an engaging talk on garden design by DP Garden Design, followed by a beautifully presented afternoon tea, served at elegantly dressed tables with crisp linens, surrounded by the charm of the garden in full bloom.

Tickets £35

name: Gardens, Landscapes & Horticulture in Art Exhibition
snippet: A city centre exhibition of local artists and glass makers.
details:
Come and admire the work of 16 talented local artists and glass makers displayed in this lovely, little-known city centre converted chapel set in pleasant gardens.

The artists include Emma Lowres, Julie Hepenstal, Jean Wheeler, Kirsten Shaw, Abigail McKern, Sue Watson, Melanie Bliss, Julie McRae, Pradeepa David, Pat Clarke, Jean Bassington, Katy McIntyre Brown, Liz Cox, Andrea Findlay, Sheila Topley, and Nadine Collingwood. All work on display will be for sale. Access via Jewry Street or St. Peter’s Street.

10am - 5pm with Preview Evening from 6 - 8pm on 29th June.

Free entry

name: Gardens, Landscapes & Horticulture in Art Exhibition
snippet: A city centre exhibition of local artists and glass makers.
details:
Come and admire the work of 16 talented local artists and glass makers displayed in this lovely, little-known city centre converted chapel set in pleasant gardens.

name: A Guided Pick Your Own Flowers experience with Featherstone Flowers

location.street: Berry Court Farm, Church Hill, Nether Wallop

snippet: A guided flower field tour with harvesting advice.
details:
A group guided tour of the flower field led by a Featherstone’s team member showing how to best harvest your flowers for maximum vase life together with information on how flowers are grown at the farm.

Tickets £35

name: Come to the Flower Field: Pick, Arrange and Journal
snippet: Pick flowers, arrange them, and explore journalling techniques.
details:
Spend a relaxed morning in the flower field at Lower Norton Flower Farm. Pick a jam jar full of seasonal British grown flowers, learn how to cut and arrange them, then use your blooms as inspiration for simple journalling techniques.

No artistic experience is needed, just come and enjoy flowers, creativity and a little time in nature.

Sessions on 1st July and 3rd July.

Tickets £49

name: Come to the Flower Field: Pick, Arrange and Journal
snippet: Pick flowers, arrange them, and explore journalling techniques.
details:
Spend a relaxed morning in the flower field at Lower Norton Flower Farm. Pick a jam jar full of seasonal British grown flowers, learn how to cut and arrange them, then use your blooms as inspiration for simple journalling techniques.

No artistic experience is needed, just come and enjoy flowers, creativity and a little time in nature.

Sessions on 1st July and 3rd July.

Tickets £49

name: The Market Garden Dinner
snippet: A long-table dinner in a working market garden.
details:
Come and enjoy a delicious supper sitting at long tables in the middle of a market garden which has produced the vegetables on your plate.

With magnificent views and amazing food and drinks, this is an exciting, new, fabulous community event.

Tickets £50

name: Community Allotment Gardening Open Day
snippet: Tour allotments, meet volunteers and learn gardening skills.
details:
Tour the allotments with volunteers, ask your allotment questions, observe the work in progress and even give a helping hand if desired.

There will also be a seed identification activity.

Free entry

name: Summer Hand Tied Bouquet Workshop
snippet: Learn to create a hand-tied bouquet from fresh garden flowers.
details:
Learn how to pick and arrange a beautiful, fresh bouquet of garden flowers.

After picking blooms and enjoying tea and cake, you will be shown how to make a showstopping hand-tied bouquet to take home.

Tickets £65

name: Dinner on the Terrace in partnership with Hampshire Pantry
snippet: An elegant dinner with cocktails, wine and garden views.
details:
The evening will begin with botanical cocktails and canapés in one of the magnificent gardens of The Deane House.

Enjoy a 2 course dinner with wines on the terrace, crafted from locally grown ingredients.

Tickets £75

name: Hampshire Market Garden Summer Gala
snippet: Festival finale with food, music and garden stalls.
details:
For this final weekend of The Garden Festival, Hampshire Market Garden is hosting a special Summer Gala.

The gardens will be open to browse, with stalls, live music, and local food and drink.

Free entry
